items.title: Federal court vacates SNAP 'food restriction' waivers in five states, threatening broader state bans
items.summary: On June 22, 2026, a federal district court vacated USDA SNAP waivers that let Colorado, Iowa, Nebraska, Tennessee, and West Virginia block purchases like soda and candy, finding USDA lacked authority to waive Congress’s definition of eligible food. USDA says it has approved 18 states for SNAP food restriction waivers, so the ruling could influence other states’ planned restrictions even though it directly addressed only five waivers.

items.title: USDA lists 18 states approved for SNAP food restriction waivers
items.summary: USDA’s “Make America Healthy Again” page says it has approved 18 states to restrict certain items from purchase with SNAP benefits. The page explicitly lists Arkansas, Colorado, Florida, Idaho, Indiana, Iowa, Louisiana, Nebraska, Oklahoma, Texas, Utah, West Virginia, Missouri, North Dakota, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and Hawaii.

items.title: Medicaid work-requirement rule: public comments due July 31, 2026; requirements take effect January 1, 2027
items.summary: A policy explainer notes CMS issued an interim final rule on June 1, 2026 creating a national framework for Medicaid “community engagement” (work) requirements, with a 60-day comment period open until July 31, 2026. The rule is described as taking effect January 1, 2027, with exemptions including people under 19 or over 64, people receiving SSI/SSDI, and certain caregivers and medically frail individuals.

items.title: SSA schedule shows SSI paid July 1, 2026 (and an August date appears on the calendar)
items.summary: SSA’s official 2026–2027 benefit payment schedule shows Supplemental Security Income (SSI) paid on July 1, 2026. The same SSA calendar also shows an August 2026 payment date of August 31, 2026.

items.title: WIC income eligibility guidelines updated for July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027
items.summary: USDA’s Federal Register notice sets WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027, tied to 185% of federal poverty guidelines. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the 185% guideline is $29,526/year ($2,461/month) for a 1-person household and $61,050/year ($5,088/month) for a 4-person household.

items.title: WIC cash-value benefit (fruits/vegetables) levels listed at $26/$47/$52 per month (FY 2024 inflation-adjusted levels)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food packages page lists cash-value benefit (CVB) levels at the time of rule implementation due to inflation (FY 2024) as $26 per month for children, $47 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for breastfeeding participants. These amounts are the monthly produce benefits used for fresh, frozen, or canned fruits and vegetables.

items.title: Federal FAFSA deadline for 2026–27 is June 30, 2027 (apply early for state/school aid)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid guidance for the 2026–27 cycle says the FAFSA remains open through June 30, 2027 for aid covering July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. Many states and colleges run out of grant money earlier, so filing well before the federal deadline can matter for total aid.

items.title: SSA posts its July 2026 Monthly Information Package for advocates
items.summary: SSA posted a “July Monthly Information Package” on June 22, 2026 to highlight new Social Security-related articles and updates for advocates. It’s a recurring SSA resource used to share timely program information and announcements.
